However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}}

[[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nomi Kofun Cluster | native_name = 能美古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akitsune_1_koen enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akitsuneyama No.1 Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nomi Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|50|N|136|30|34|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 134,942 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nomi Kofun Cluster'''|能美古墳群||}} consists of five groups of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s in [[:en:Terai, Ishikawa|Terai, Ishikawa]] (now part of the city of [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]]) in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Two of these ''kofun'' clusters were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975, with the Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster added in 1998 and expanded in 2000, and the Teraiyama and Nishiyama clusters added in 2013.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162601/5|title= 能美古墳群|trans-title= |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Nomi Kofun Cluster consists of total of 62 ''kofun'' scattered in the plains of Nomi city. The tumuli are in various shapes and sizes and include two keyhole-shaped tombs ({{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above). The construction dates of these tumuli span the range of the Kofun period, from the 3rd to the 6th centuries AD. A large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered from various of these tumuli, and include six "bell-mirrors", bronze weapons, armor and horse fittings, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" |+ types of ''kofun'' in the Nomi Kofun Cluster (surviving in parentheses) |- ! !! ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' !! {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!! ''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''!! {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=''hōfun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!! Indeterminate !! Total |- | '''Teraiyama Kofun Cluster'''
寺井山古墳群 || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 6 (0) || 7 (1) |- | '''Wadayama Kofun Cluster'''
和田山古墳群 || 1 (0) || - || 14 (13) || 2 (0) || 6 (3) || 23 (19) |- | '''Matsujiyama Kofun Cluster'''
末寺山古墳群 || - || 3 (3) || 6 (6) || - || 3 (0) || 12 (9) |- | '''Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster'''
秋常山古墳群 || 1 (1) || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 2 (2) |- | '''Nishiyama Kofun Cluster'''
西山古墳群 || - || - || 18 (13) || - || - || 18 (13) |- | ''Totals'' ||2 (2) ||3 (3)|| 39 (33) || 3(3) || 15 (3) || 62 (44) |} ==Akitsuyama Kofun Cluster== The Akitsuneyama site consists of two ''kofun''. Kofun No.1 is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), and was built at the end of the early Kofun period in the 4th century AD. It has a length of 141 meters, making it the largest ancient tomb in the Hokuriku region. Kofun No.2 is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), and dates from the middle of the middle Kofun period. The site is located on a slight elevation above the plain of the [[:en:Tedori River|Tedori River]], within view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. The site was severely damaged by locals, who pillaged the ancient tombs for iron artifacts, which were given to the authorities to be melted down as part of the war effort. However, Kofun No.1 was spared from pillaging despite its huge size, as it was not discovered until 1984. It was located within the grounds of the Aritsune Hachiman Shrine, and was considered a natural hill covered by a dense growth of trees. When the trees were cleared away, the ''kofun'' was found to have a three-trier structure. The bottom trier had a length of 141 meters, with a 108-meter circular portion, 67 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 19 meters. The middle trier had a length of 121.5 meters, with a 90.5 meter circular portion, 46.5 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 16 meters. The third trier was on the circular portion only, extending its height by 4 meters. In addition, there was a protrusion on the east side of the ''kofun'', measuring 8 by 16 meters extending out from the second trier. The entire kofun was covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' The interior of the ''kofun'' has not been excavated, but the presence of a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed by [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]], with a wooden [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] exceeding 8 meters in length, with the presence of metallic [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}}

寺井山古墳群 || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 6 (0) || 7 (1) |- | '''Wadayama Kofun Cluster'''
和田山古墳群 || 1 (0) || - || 14 (13) || 2 (0) || 6 (3) || 23 (19) |- | '''Matsujiyama Kofun Cluster'''
末寺山古墳群 || - || 3 (3) || 6 (6) || - || 3 (0) || 12 (9) |- | '''Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster'''
秋常山古墳群 || 1 (1) || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 2 (2) |- | '''Nishiyama Kofun Cluster'''
西山古墳群 || - || - || 18 (13) || - || - || 18 (13) |- | ''Totals'' ||2 (2) ||3 (3)|| 39 (33) || 3(3) || 15 (3) || 62 (44) |} ==Akitsuyama Kofun Cluster== The Akitsuneyama site consists of two ''kofun''. Kofun No.1 is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), and was built at the end of the early Kofun period in the 4th century AD. It has a length of 141 meters, making it the largest ancient tomb in the Hokuriku region. Kofun No.2 is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), and dates from the middle of the middle Kofun period. The site is located on a slight elevation above the plain of the [[:en:Tedori River|Tedori River]], within view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. The site was severely damaged by locals, who pillaged the ancient tombs for iron artifacts, which were given to the authorities to be melted down as part of the war effort. However, Kofun No.1 was spared from pillaging despite its huge size, as it was not discovered until 1984. It was located within the grounds of the Aritsune Hachiman Shrine, and was considered a natural hill covered by a dense growth of trees. When the trees were cleared away, the ''kofun'' was found to have a three-trier structure. The bottom trier had a length of 141 meters, with a 108-meter circular portion, 67 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 19 meters. The middle trier had a length of 121.5 meters, with a 90.5 meter circular portion, 46.5 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 16 meters. The third trier was on the circular portion only, extending its height by 4 meters. In addition, there was a protrusion on the east side of the ''kofun'', measuring 8 by 16 meters extending out from the second trier. The entire kofun was covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' The interior of the ''kofun'' has not been excavated, but the presence of a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed by [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]], with a wooden [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] exceeding 8 meters in length, with the presence of metallic [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nomi Kofun Cluster | native_name = 能美古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akitsune_1_koen enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akitsuneyama No.1 Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nomi Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|50|N|136|30|34|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 134,942 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nomi Kofun Cluster'''|能美古墳群||}} consists of five groups of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s in [[:en:Terai, Ishikawa|Terai, Ishikawa]] (now part of the city of [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]]) in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Two of these ''kofun'' clusters were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975, with the Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster added in 1998 and expanded in 2000, and the Teraiyama and Nishiyama clusters added in 2013.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162601/5|title= 能美古墳群|trans-title= |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Nomi Kofun Cluster consists of total of 62 ''kofun'' scattered in the plains of Nomi city. The tumuli are in various shapes and sizes and include two keyhole-shaped tombs ({{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above). The construction dates of these tumuli span the range of the Kofun period, from the 3rd to the 6th centuries AD. A large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered from various of these tumuli, and include six "bell-mirrors", bronze weapons, armor and horse fittings, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" |+ types of ''kofun'' in the Nomi Kofun Cluster (surviving in parentheses) |- ! !! ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' !! {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!! ''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''!! {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=''hōfun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!! Indeterminate !! Total |- | '''Teraiyama Kofun Cluster'''

和田山古墳群 || 1 (0) || - || 14 (13) || 2 (0) || 6 (3) || 23 (19) |- | '''Matsujiyama Kofun Cluster'''

秋常山古墳群 || 1 (1) || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 2 (2) |- | '''Nishiyama Kofun Cluster'''

Kofun No.1 is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), and was built at the end of the early Kofun period in the 4th century AD. It has a length of 141 meters, making it the largest ancient tomb in the Hokuriku region. Kofun No.2 is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), and dates from the middle of the middle Kofun period. The site is located on a slight elevation above the plain of the [[:en:Tedori River|Tedori River]], within view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. The site was severely damaged by locals, who pillaged the ancient tombs for iron artifacts, which were given to the authorities to be melted down as part of the war effort. However, Kofun No.1 was spared from pillaging despite its huge size, as it was not discovered until 1984. It was located within the grounds of the Aritsune Hachiman Shrine, and was considered a natural hill covered by a dense growth of trees. When the trees were cleared away, the ''kofun'' was found to have a three-trier structure. The bottom trier had a length of 141 meters, with a 108-meter circular portion, 67 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 19 meters. The middle trier had a length of 121.5 meters, with a 90.5 meter circular portion, 46.5 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 16 meters. The third trier was on the circular portion only, extending its height by 4 meters. In addition, there was a protrusion on the east side of the ''kofun'', measuring 8 by 16 meters extending out from the second trier. The entire kofun was covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' The interior of the ''kofun'' has not been excavated, but the presence of a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed by [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]], with a wooden [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] exceeding 8 meters in length, with the presence of metallic [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nomi Kofun Cluster | native_name = 能美古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akitsune_1_koen enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akitsuneyama No.1 Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nomi Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|50|N|136|30|34|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 134,942 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nomi Kofun Cluster'''|能美古墳群||}} consists of five groups of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s in [[:en:Terai, Ishikawa|Terai, Ishikawa]] (now part of the city of [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]]) in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Two of these ''kofun'' clusters were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975, with the Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster added in 1998 and expanded in 2000, and the Teraiyama and Nishiyama clusters added in 2013.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162601/5|title= 能美古墳群|trans-title= |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Nomi Kofun Cluster consists of total of 62 ''kofun'' scattered in the plains of Nomi city. The tumuli are in various shapes and sizes and include two keyhole-shaped tombs ({{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above). The construction dates of these tumuli span the range of the Kofun period, from the 3rd to the 6th centuries AD. A large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered from various of these tumuli, and include six "bell-mirrors", bronze weapons, armor and horse fittings, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" |+ types of ''kofun'' in the Nomi Kofun Cluster (surviving in parentheses) |- ! !! Indeterminate !! Total |- | '''Teraiyama Kofun Cluster'''
寺井山古墳群 || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 6 (0) || 7 (1) |- | '''Wadayama Kofun Cluster'''
和田山古墳群 || 1 (0) || - || 14 (13) || 2 (0) || 6 (3) || 23 (19) |- | '''Matsujiyama Kofun Cluster'''
末寺山古墳群 || - || 3 (3) || 6 (6) || - || 3 (0) || 12 (9) |- | '''Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster'''
秋常山古墳群 || 1 (1) || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 2 (2) |- | '''Nishiyama Kofun Cluster'''
西山古墳群 || - || - || 18 (13) || - || - || 18 (13) |- | ''Totals'' ||2 (2) ||3 (3)|| 39 (33) || 3(3) || 15 (3) || 62 (44) |} ==Akitsuyama Kofun Cluster== The Akitsuneyama site consists of two ''kofun''. Kofun No.1 is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), and was built at the end of the early Kofun period in the 4th century AD. It has a length of 141 meters, making it the largest ancient tomb in the Hokuriku region. Kofun No.2 is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), and dates from the middle of the middle Kofun period. The site is located on a slight elevation above the plain of the [[:en:Tedori River|Tedori River]], within view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. The site was severely damaged by locals, who pillaged the ancient tombs for iron artifacts, which were given to the authorities to be melted down as part of the war effort. However, Kofun No.1 was spared from pillaging despite its huge size, as it was not discovered until 1984. It was located within the grounds of the Aritsune Hachiman Shrine, and was considered a natural hill covered by a dense growth of trees. When the trees were cleared away, the ''kofun'' was found to have a three-trier structure. The bottom trier had a length of 141 meters, with a 108-meter circular portion, 67 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 19 meters. The middle trier had a length of 121.5 meters, with a 90.5 meter circular portion, 46.5 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 16 meters. The third trier was on the circular portion only, extending its height by 4 meters. In addition, there was a protrusion on the east side of the ''kofun'', measuring 8 by 16 meters extending out from the second trier. The entire kofun was covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' The interior of the ''kofun'' has not been excavated, but the presence of a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed by [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]], with a wooden [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] exceeding 8 meters in length, with the presence of metallic [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

A large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered from various of these tumuli, and include six "bell-mirrors", bronze weapons, armor and horse fittings, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" |+ types of ''kofun'' in the Nomi Kofun Cluster (surviving in parentheses) |- ! !! ''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''!! {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=''hōfun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!! Indeterminate !! Total |- | '''Teraiyama Kofun Cluster'''
寺井山古墳群 || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 6 (0) || 7 (1) |- | '''Wadayama Kofun Cluster'''
和田山古墳群 || 1 (0) || - || 14 (13) || 2 (0) || 6 (3) || 23 (19) |- | '''Matsujiyama Kofun Cluster'''
末寺山古墳群 || - || 3 (3) || 6 (6) || - || 3 (0) || 12 (9) |- | '''Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster'''
秋常山古墳群 || 1 (1) || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 2 (2) |- | '''Nishiyama Kofun Cluster'''
西山古墳群 || - || - || 18 (13) || - || - || 18 (13) |- | ''Totals'' ||2 (2) ||3 (3)|| 39 (33) || 3(3) || 15 (3) || 62 (44) |} ==Akitsuyama Kofun Cluster== The Akitsuneyama site consists of two ''kofun''. Kofun No.1 is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), and was built at the end of the early Kofun period in the 4th century AD. It has a length of 141 meters, making it the largest ancient tomb in the Hokuriku region. Kofun No.2 is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), and dates from the middle of the middle Kofun period. The site is located on a slight elevation above the plain of the [[:en:Tedori River|Tedori River]], within view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. The site was severely damaged by locals, who pillaged the ancient tombs for iron artifacts, which were given to the authorities to be melted down as part of the war effort. However, Kofun No.1 was spared from pillaging despite its huge size, as it was not discovered until 1984. It was located within the grounds of the Aritsune Hachiman Shrine, and was considered a natural hill covered by a dense growth of trees. When the trees were cleared away, the ''kofun'' was found to have a three-trier structure. The bottom trier had a length of 141 meters, with a 108-meter circular portion, 67 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 19 meters. The middle trier had a length of 121.5 meters, with a 90.5 meter circular portion, 46.5 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 16 meters. The third trier was on the circular portion only, extending its height by 4 meters. In addition, there was a protrusion on the east side of the ''kofun'', measuring 8 by 16 meters extending out from the second trier. The entire kofun was covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' The interior of the ''kofun'' has not been excavated, but the presence of a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed by [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]], with a wooden [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] exceeding 8 meters in length, with the presence of metallic [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nomi Kofun Cluster | native_name = 能美古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akitsune_1_koen enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akitsuneyama No.1 Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nomi Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|50|N|136|30|34|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 134,942 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nomi Kofun Cluster'''|能美古墳群||}} consists of five groups of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s in [[:en:Terai, Ishikawa|Terai, Ishikawa]] (now part of the city of [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]]) in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Two of these ''kofun'' clusters were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975, with the Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster added in 1998 and expanded in 2000, and the Teraiyama and Nishiyama clusters added in 2013.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162601/5|title= 能美古墳群|trans-title= |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Nomi Kofun Cluster consists of total of 62 ''kofun'' scattered in the plains of Nomi city. The tumuli are in various shapes and sizes and include two keyhole-shaped tombs ({{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above). The construction dates of these tumuli span the range of the Kofun period, from the 3rd to the 6th centuries AD. A large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered from various of these tumuli, and include six "bell-mirrors", bronze weapons, armor and horse fittings, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" |+ types of ''kofun'' in the Nomi Kofun Cluster (surviving in parentheses) |- ! !! ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' !! {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!! ''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''!! {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=''hōfun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!! Indeterminate !! Total |- | '''Teraiyama Kofun Cluster'''
寺井山古墳群 || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 6 (0) || 7 (1) |- | '''Wadayama Kofun Cluster'''
和田山古墳群 || 1 (0) || - || 14 (13) || 2 (0) || 6 (3) || 23 (19) |- | '''Matsujiyama Kofun Cluster'''
末寺山古墳群 || - || 3 (3) || 6 (6) || - || 3 (0) || 12 (9) |- | '''Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster'''
秋常山古墳群 || 1 (1) || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 2 (2) |- | '''Nishiyama Kofun Cluster'''
西山古墳群 || - || - || 18 (13) || - || - || 18 (13) |- | ''Totals'' ||2 (2) ||3 (3)|| 39 (33) || 3(3) || 15 (3) || 62 (44) |} ==Akitsuyama Kofun Cluster== The Akitsuneyama site consists of two ''kofun''. Kofun No.1 is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), and was built at the end of the early Kofun period in the 4th century AD. It has a length of 141 meters, making it the largest ancient tomb in the Hokuriku region. Kofun No.2 is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), and dates from the middle of the middle Kofun period. The site is located on a slight elevation above the plain of the [[:en:Tedori River|Tedori River]], within view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. The site was severely damaged by locals, who pillaged the ancient tombs for iron artifacts, which were given to the authorities to be melted down as part of the war effort. However, Kofun No.1 was spared from pillaging despite its huge size, as it was not discovered until 1984. It was located within the grounds of the Aritsune Hachiman Shrine, and was considered a natural hill covered by a dense growth of trees. When the trees were cleared away, the ''kofun'' was found to have a three-trier structure. The bottom trier had a length of 141 meters, with a 108-meter circular portion, 67 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 19 meters. The middle trier had a length of 121.5 meters, with a 90.5 meter circular portion, 46.5 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 16 meters. The third trier was on the circular portion only, extending its height by 4 meters. In addition, there was a protrusion on the east side of the ''kofun'', measuring 8 by 16 meters extending out from the second trier. The entire kofun was covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' The interior of the ''kofun'' has not been excavated, but the presence of a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed by [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]], with a wooden [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] exceeding 8 meters in length, with the presence of metallic [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nomi Kofun Cluster | native_name = 能美古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akitsune_1_koen enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akitsuneyama No.1 Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nomi Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|50|N|136|30|34|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 134,942 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nomi Kofun Cluster'''|能美古墳群||}} consists of five groups of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s in [[:en:Terai, Ishikawa|Terai, Ishikawa]] (now part of the city of [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]]) in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Two of these ''kofun'' clusters were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975, with the Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster added in 1998 and expanded in 2000, and the Teraiyama and Nishiyama clusters added in 2013.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162601/5|title= 能美古墳群|trans-title= |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Nomi Kofun Cluster consists of total of 62 ''kofun'' scattered in the plains of Nomi city. The tumuli are in various shapes and sizes and include two keyhole-shaped tombs ({{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above). The construction dates of these tumuli span the range of the Kofun period, from the 3rd to the 6th centuries AD. A large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered from various of these tumuli, and include six "bell-mirrors", bronze weapons, armor and horse fittings, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" |+ types of ''kofun'' in the Nomi Kofun Cluster (surviving in parentheses) |- ! !! ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' !! {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!! Indeterminate !! Total |- | '''Teraiyama Kofun Cluster'''
寺井山古墳群 || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 6 (0) || 7 (1) |- | '''Wadayama Kofun Cluster'''
和田山古墳群 || 1 (0) || - || 14 (13) || 2 (0) || 6 (3) || 23 (19) |- | '''Matsujiyama Kofun Cluster'''
末寺山古墳群 || - || 3 (3) || 6 (6) || - || 3 (0) || 12 (9) |- | '''Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster'''
秋常山古墳群 || 1 (1) || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 2 (2) |- | '''Nishiyama Kofun Cluster'''
西山古墳群 || - || - || 18 (13) || - || - || 18 (13) |- | ''Totals'' ||2 (2) ||3 (3)|| 39 (33) || 3(3) || 15 (3) || 62 (44) |} ==Akitsuyama Kofun Cluster== The Akitsuneyama site consists of two ''kofun''. Kofun No.1 is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), and was built at the end of the early Kofun period in the 4th century AD. It has a length of 141 meters, making it the largest ancient tomb in the Hokuriku region. Kofun No.2 is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), and dates from the middle of the middle Kofun period. The site is located on a slight elevation above the plain of the [[:en:Tedori River|Tedori River]], within view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. The site was severely damaged by locals, who pillaged the ancient tombs for iron artifacts, which were given to the authorities to be melted down as part of the war effort. However, Kofun No.1 was spared from pillaging despite its huge size, as it was not discovered until 1984. It was located within the grounds of the Aritsune Hachiman Shrine, and was considered a natural hill covered by a dense growth of trees. When the trees were cleared away, the ''kofun'' was found to have a three-trier structure. The bottom trier had a length of 141 meters, with a 108-meter circular portion, 67 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 19 meters. The middle trier had a length of 121.5 meters, with a 90.5 meter circular portion, 46.5 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 16 meters. The third trier was on the circular portion only, extending its height by 4 meters. In addition, there was a protrusion on the east side of the ''kofun'', measuring 8 by 16 meters extending out from the second trier. The entire kofun was covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' The interior of the ''kofun'' has not been excavated, but the presence of a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed by [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]], with a wooden [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] exceeding 8 meters in length, with the presence of metallic [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

A large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered from various of these tumuli, and include six "bell-mirrors", bronze weapons, armor and horse fittings, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" |+ types of ''kofun'' in the Nomi Kofun Cluster (surviving in parentheses) |- ! !! ''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''!! {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=''hōfun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!! Indeterminate !! Total |- | '''Teraiyama Kofun Cluster'''
寺井山古墳群 || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 6 (0) || 7 (1) |- | '''Wadayama Kofun Cluster'''
和田山古墳群 || 1 (0) || - || 14 (13) || 2 (0) || 6 (3) || 23 (19) |- | '''Matsujiyama Kofun Cluster'''
末寺山古墳群 || - || 3 (3) || 6 (6) || - || 3 (0) || 12 (9) |- | '''Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster'''
秋常山古墳群 || 1 (1) || - || - || 1 (1) || - || 2 (2) |- | '''Nishiyama Kofun Cluster'''
西山古墳群 || - || - || 18 (13) || - || - || 18 (13) |- | ''Totals'' ||2 (2) ||3 (3)|| 39 (33) || 3(3) || 15 (3) || 62 (44) |} ==Akitsuyama Kofun Cluster== The Akitsuneyama site consists of two ''kofun''. Kofun No.1 is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), and was built at the end of the early Kofun period in the 4th century AD. It has a length of 141 meters, making it the largest ancient tomb in the Hokuriku region. Kofun No.2 is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), and dates from the middle of the middle Kofun period. The site is located on a slight elevation above the plain of the [[:en:Tedori River|Tedori River]], within view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. The site was severely damaged by locals, who pillaged the ancient tombs for iron artifacts, which were given to the authorities to be melted down as part of the war effort. However, Kofun No.1 was spared from pillaging despite its huge size, as it was not discovered until 1984. It was located within the grounds of the Aritsune Hachiman Shrine, and was considered a natural hill covered by a dense growth of trees. When the trees were cleared away, the ''kofun'' was found to have a three-trier structure. The bottom trier had a length of 141 meters, with a 108-meter circular portion, 67 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 19 meters. The middle trier had a length of 121.5 meters, with a 90.5 meter circular portion, 46.5 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 16 meters. The third trier was on the circular portion only, extending its height by 4 meters. In addition, there was a protrusion on the east side of the ''kofun'', measuring 8 by 16 meters extending out from the second trier. The entire kofun was covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' The interior of the ''kofun'' has not been excavated, but the presence of a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed by [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]], with a wooden [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] exceeding 8 meters in length, with the presence of metallic [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nomi Kofun Cluster | native_name = 能美古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akitsune_1_koen enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akitsuneyama No.1 Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nomi Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|50|N|136|30|34|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 134,942 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nomi Kofun Cluster'''|能美古墳群||}} consists of five groups of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s in [[:en:Terai, Ishikawa|Terai, Ishikawa]] (now part of the city of [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]]) in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Two of these ''kofun'' clusters were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975, with the Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster added in 1998 and expanded in 2000, and the Teraiyama and Nishiyama clusters added in 2013.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162601/5|title= 能美古墳群|trans-title= |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Nomi Kofun Cluster consists of total of 62 ''kofun'' scattered in the plains of Nomi city. The tumuli are in various shapes and sizes and include two keyhole-shaped tombs ({{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above). The construction dates of these tumuli span the range of the Kofun period, from the 3rd to the 6th centuries AD. A large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered from various of these tumuli, and include six "bell-mirrors", bronze weapons, armor and horse fittings, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" |+ types of ''kofun'' in the Nomi Kofun Cluster (surviving in parentheses) |- ! !! ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' !! {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!! ''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''!! {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=''hōfun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!! Indeterminate !! Total |- | '''Teraiyama Kofun Cluster'''

和田山古墳群 || 1 (0) || - || 14 (13) || 2 (0) || 6 (3) || 23 (19) |- | '''Matsujiyama Kofun Cluster'''

Kofun No.1 is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), and was built at the end of the early Kofun period in the 4th century AD. It has a length of 141 meters, making it the largest ancient tomb in the Hokuriku region. Kofun No.2 is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), and dates from the middle of the middle Kofun period. The site is located on a slight elevation above the plain of the [[:en:Tedori River|Tedori River]], within view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. The site was severely damaged by locals, who pillaged the ancient tombs for iron artifacts, which were given to the authorities to be melted down as part of the war effort. However, Kofun No.1 was spared from pillaging despite its huge size, as it was not discovered until 1984. It was located within the grounds of the Aritsune Hachiman Shrine, and was considered a natural hill covered by a dense growth of trees. When the trees were cleared away, the ''kofun'' was found to have a three-trier structure. The bottom trier had a length of 141 meters, with a 108-meter circular portion, 67 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 19 meters. The middle trier had a length of 121.5 meters, with a 90.5 meter circular portion, 46.5 meter wide rectangular portion and a height of 16 meters. The third trier was on the circular portion only, extending its height by 4 meters. In addition, there was a protrusion on the east side of the ''kofun'', measuring 8 by 16 meters extending out from the second trier. The entire kofun was covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' The interior of the ''kofun'' has not been excavated, but the presence of a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed by [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]], with a wooden [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] exceeding 8 meters in length, with the presence of metallic [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

However, radar also indicated the possibly of a second burial chamber located in the vicinity of the anterior part mound top and neck. Kofun No.2 was excavated in 2001. It had dimensions of 32.5 meters by 27 meters, with a maximum height of 5.4 meters. Much of it had been destroyed by farmers for dirt. Numerous ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including beads and fragments of swords and other metal objects. ==Gallery== Akitsune 1 frm Wadayama 5.jpg|Akitsuyama from Wadayama 秋常山古墳 - panoramio.jpg| Panorama of Akitsuyama Kofun cluster Akitsune 1 Fukiishi.jpg|Fukiishi on Akitsuyama No.1 Akitsuneyama kofungun 1975.jpg|Map of Akitsuyama Site ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akitsuyama Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-12.html Ishikawa Prefectural site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nomi.ishikawa.jp/www/contents/1556174850772/index.html Nomi city home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} [[file:Distribution of Nomi kofungun.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Map of Nomi Kofun Cluster site]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q105562368}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Nomi, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Nomi Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nomi Kofun Cluster | native_name = 能美古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akitsune_1_koen enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akitsuneyama No.1 Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nomi Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|50|N|136|30|34|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 134,942 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nomi Kofun Cluster'''|能美古墳群||}} consists of five groups of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s in [[:en:Terai, Ishikawa|Terai, Ishikawa]] (now part of the city of [[:en:Nomi, Ishikawa|Nomi, Ishikawa]]) in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Two of these ''kofun'' clusters were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975, with the Akitsuneyama Kofun Cluster added in 1998 and expanded in 2000, and the Teraiyama and Nishiyama clusters added in 2013.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162601/5|title= 能美古墳群|trans-title= |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Nomi Kofun Cluster consists of total of 62 ''kofun'' scattered in the plains of Nomi city. The tumuli are in various shapes and sizes and include two keyhole-shaped tombs ({{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above). The construction dates of these tumuli span the range of the Kofun period, from the 3rd to the 6th centuries AD. A large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered from various of these tumuli, and include six "bell-mirrors", bronze weapons, armor and horse fittings, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" |+ types of ''kofun'' in the Nomi Kofun Cluster (surviving in parentheses) |- ! !! ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' !! {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}!!
